title: NOC 22300 – Civil Engineering Technologists and Technicians | GTR Immigration
description: Civil Engineering Technologists and Technicians are in strong demand across Canada, driven by large-scale infrastructure renewal, housing construction, highway expansion, public-transit projects, and climate-resilient engineering programs nationwide. Classified under NOC 22300 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Prepare construction specifications, cost estimates & schedules
employment_requirements: Two- to three-year college diploma in civil-engineering technology (technologists)
One- to two-year diploma in civil-engineering technology (technicians)
Around two years of supervised work experience for certification
In Quebec, regulatory-body membership required for the title Professional Technologist
job_outlook: This competitive outlook still favours candidates with:
pathways: Advancement into project-management or design-coordination roles
additional_information: Canadian employers are actively recruiting civil-engineering technical professionals for:
Highway & bridge projectsMunicipal infrastructure upgradesTransit systemsWater & wastewater plantsLand-development projectsConsulting-engineering firmsGovernment public-works departments
Annual salaries typically range between $38,500–$100,100 CAD, depending on province, certification level, and project specialization.
